# Bubbles Enterprise Soffit & Fascia — Orlando, FL

> Expert soffit and fascia installation, repair, and replacement in Orlando and Central Florida.
> Licensed and insured. Serving residential and new construction since 2020.
> Phone: (407) 715-1790. Available Mon–Sat 8am–6pm. Emergency repairs available.
> Website: https://bubblesenterprise.com
> Service area: Orlando, Kissimmee, Winter Park, Winter Garden, Clermont, Sanford, Oviedo,
> Lake Mary, Apopka, Altamonte Springs, Maitland, Casselberry, Longwood, Celebration,
> St. Cloud, and 45+ Central Florida cities.

Bubbles Enterprise is a specialty contractor focused exclusively on soffit and fascia systems. The company does not perform roofing, structural framing, electrical, plumbing, or interior repairs. This narrow focus allows the team to develop deep expertise in exactly one trade. The company is a preferred contractor for KB Home and a certified partner of DR Horton, two of the largest home builders in the United States. ABC Supply authorized dealer. Over 500 homes served across Central Florida.

---

## Services

Bubbles Enterprise offers three main service categories: Soffit Repairs, Remove & Replace, and New Construction installation. All services cover both aluminum and vinyl soffit materials. Free on-site estimates are provided for every project.

### Soffit & Fascia Repair

Repairs address localized damage without replacing the entire soffit system. This is the most cost-effective option when damage is limited to specific sections.

Repair services cover the following damage types:

- Visible damage: Cracks, holes, dents, or sagging sections that expose the attic to water, insects, and wildlife.
- Water and moisture damage: Staining, rot, and moisture infiltration, common after Florida storms. Leads to mold if left untreated.
- Poor ventilation: Blocked or crushed vents cutting attic airflow, increasing cooling costs and shortening roof life.
- Pest intrusion: Birds, wasps, squirrels, or lizards entering through open soffit gaps and nesting in the attic.
- Peeling and fading: Florida UV intensity causes accelerated deterioration. Cosmetic repairs protect structure and curb appeal.
- Installation issues: Missing fasteners, panels pulling away from fascia, or improper original installation causing gaps.

Repair costs are 60-80% less than full replacement for isolated damage. Most repairs are completed within 1-2 days. Emergency same-day service is available. All repair work is covered by a 1-year workmanship warranty.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/repairs

### Remove & Replace

Remove & Replace (R&R) is a complete soffit system replacement. Old materials are fully removed and new aluminum or vinyl is installed. This service is appropriate when damage is extensive, the soffit is past its useful life, or the homeowner wants to upgrade materials.

When complete replacement is necessary:

- Extensive damage: Widespread rot, structural damage, or multiple failing sections beyond repair.
- Age and obsolescence: Old wood soffits reaching end of life or outdated materials needing upgrade.
- Poor ventilation system: Inadequate or non-existent ventilation causing energy efficiency issues.
- Home renovation: Upgrading exterior appearance or matching new siding installation.
- Energy efficiency: Upgrading to modern materials for better insulation and lower energy costs.
- Long-term investment: Choosing maintenance-free materials for decades of worry-free protection.

Benefits of full replacement include maximum longevity (vinyl 10+ years, aluminum 20-30 years), enhanced energy efficiency through proper ventilation, complete protection against moisture and pests, increased home value (75-85% ROI), and current building code compliance.

Installation takes 2-5 days depending on home size and complexity. Fascia wrap is included with the service.

Pricing for Remove & Replace:
- 1-story homes: $6 per linear foot labor
- 2-story homes: $7 per linear foot labor
- Material (overhang rate) is added on top of labor (see Pricing section below)

Energy savings from proper R&R installation: $350-700 per year on a typical 2,000 sq ft home. Summer cooling savings average $200-400/year. Winter heating efficiency savings average $150-300/year. Proper ventilation can prevent $5,000-15,000 in potential moisture/mold damage.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/remove-replace

### New Construction

Professional soffit and fascia installation for new homes, additions, and garages. Coordinated with the construction timeline. Every installation is IRC Section 806 compliant for attic ventilation.

The new construction process has six steps:

1. Blueprint Review: Plans are reviewed to calculate exact ventilation requirements, material quantities, and problem areas before work begins.
2. Ventilation Design: A balanced intake/exhaust system is designed per IRC 806 and documented for the building inspector.
3. Material Delivery: Aluminum or vinyl delivered on the builder's schedule, not left on-site for weeks.
4. Installation: The crew works clean, coordinates with other trades, and installs to code.
5. Inspection-Ready: The crew stays on site until the inspector signs off. Adjustments are handled immediately.
6. Handover & Warranty: Full documentation, warranty certificate, and ongoing support.

Construction phases the company coordinates with:
- Pre-Drywall: Coordinate with framing completion, plan ventilation layout, ensure attic access, work alongside electrical for soffit lighting.
- Exterior Completion: Install after roofing, coordinate with siding, complete before exterior trim, align colors with paint plan.
- Final Inspection: Verify IRC 806 ventilation ratios, confirm vent airflow, quality walkthrough, deliver warranty documentation.

Why builders choose this company:
- Passes inspection the first time. Ventilation is designed to IRC Section 806 before installation begins. Documentation is provided for the inspector on arrival.
- Ventilation that works: Balanced intake and exhaust calculated by attic square footage. Proper airflow prevents moisture, extends roof shingle life by 20-30%, and reduces HVAC costs.
- Lower energy bills from day one: Soffit ventilation designed alongside insulation and HVAC results in 15-25% cooling cost reduction.
- Materials that last decades: Aluminum lasts 20-30 years; vinyl is a solid budget option at 10-15 years.
- No callbacks: Installations are right the first time, preventing mold complaints, pest entry, fallen panels, and warranty claims.
- Works on the builder's schedule: Pre-drywall, post-roofing, between trades. Coordination via text or phone.

New construction pricing:
- Labor: $4 per linear foot (all stories)
- Material (overhang rate) added on top (see Pricing section below)

New construction is 25-35% less expensive than Remove & Replace because there is no demolition involved. Installation speed is approximately 40% faster than R&R.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/new-construction

### Emergency Repair

Same-day response for urgent soffit issues. Emergency situations include animal intrusion (raccoons, squirrels, rats entering the attic through open holes), storm damage (panels ripped off by wind, exposing the attic), and large open holes that create immediate pest or water entry risk.

Emergency repair requests can be submitted through the website form or by calling (407) 715-1790.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/contact/emergency

---

## Pricing

Bubbles Enterprise provides an online calculator for instant project estimates. The pricing model uses linear feet (LF) multiplied by the sum of two rates: an overhang material rate and a labor rate. The formula is: Total = LF x (Overhang Rate + Labor Rate).

### Overhang Material Rates (per linear foot)

Overhang rates depend on the depth of the overhang (how far the soffit extends from the wall) and the material chosen.

Aluminum soffit:
- Up to 12 inches overhang: $4.00/LF
- 13 to 24 inches overhang: $5.00/LF
- 25 to 36 inches overhang: $7.00/LF
- Over 36 inches: Requires on-site structural review (not available online)

Vinyl soffit:
- Up to 12 inches overhang: $3.50/LF
- 13 to 24 inches overhang: $4.50/LF
- 25 to 36 inches overhang: $6.50/LF
- Over 36 inches: Requires on-site structural review (not available online)

### Labor Rates (per linear foot)

- New Construction: $4.00/LF
- Remove & Replace, 1-story: $6.00/LF
- Remove & Replace, 2-story: $7.00/LF

### Example Calculations

A 1-story home with 150 linear feet, 16-inch overhang (falls in 13-24 range), aluminum, Remove & Replace:
- Overhang rate: $5.00/LF
- Labor rate: $6.00/LF
- Total: 150 x ($5.00 + $6.00) = 150 x $11.00 = $1,650.00

A new construction home with 200 linear feet, 12-inch overhang, aluminum:
- Overhang rate: $4.00/LF
- Labor rate: $4.00/LF
- Total: 200 x ($4.00 + $4.00) = 200 x $8.00 = $1,600.00

### Deposit Schedule

- Projects up to $2,500: 20% deposit required
- Projects $2,501 to $4,000: $500 flat deposit
- Projects over $4,000: 30% deposit required

Final balance is due immediately upon completion and walkthrough, before the crew leaves the property. Payment accepted via check, Zelle, or cash.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/calculator

---

## Materials

Bubbles Enterprise installs two soffit materials: aluminum and vinyl. The company recommends the appropriate material based on the home's exposure, architecture, and budget.

### Aluminum Soffit

Aluminum is the professional standard for Florida homes. It is the material recommended for most projects.

Properties:
- Lifespan: 20-30 years in Florida's climate
- Fire resistance: Non-combustible (fire rated)
- Hurricane resistance: Hurricane rated, will not warp in Florida heat
- Rot resistance: 100%
- Pest resistance: Very good (cannot be chewed through by animals)
- Maintenance: None required
- Color options: 10 premium colors, 2 typically kept in stock
- Recyclable: 100%
- Manufacturer warranty: Up to 30 years available
- Availability: In stock

Aluminum is the best choice when maximum durability is the priority, when the soffit faces south or west (high UV and heat exposure), when there is a history of pest or animal intrusion, for new construction or full replacement projects, when the longest warranty is desired, or when gutters are attached to the fascia.

### Vinyl Soffit

Vinyl is a cost-effective alternative appropriate for specific situations.

Properties:
- Lifespan: 15-20 years under normal conditions; south-facing vinyl in full Florida sun may start fading and becoming brittle in 10-12 years
- Fire resistance: Will burn (not fire rated)
- Hurricane resistance: Adequate for most storms
- Heat tolerance: May warp above 140 degrees Fahrenheit
- Rot resistance: 100%
- Pest resistance: Moderate (can be chewed through by some animals)
- Maintenance: Occasional cleaning recommended
- Color options: Wider variety than aluminum (20+ colors)
- Recyclable: No
- Manufacturer warranty: 10-15 years typical
- Availability: Order lead time 1-3 days

Vinyl is acceptable when budget is the primary constraint, for shaded north-facing locations, for shorter-term ownership (selling in 5-10 years), or when matching existing vinyl on the house.

Vinyl should be avoided on south-facing or west-facing soffit in Florida due to real heat warping risk.

### Head-to-Head Comparison (Florida Conditions, 2026)

| Category | Aluminum | Vinyl | Winner |
|---|---|---|---|
| Lifespan (Florida) | 20-30 years | 15-20 years | Aluminum |
| Hurricane resistance | Hurricane rated | Adequate for most storms | Aluminum |
| Fire resistance | Non-combustible | Will burn | Aluminum |
| Heat tolerance (Florida) | No warping | May warp above 140F | Aluminum |
| Rot resistance | 100% | 100% | Tie |
| Pest resistance | Very good | Moderate (can be chewed) | Aluminum |
| Maintenance | None required | Occasional cleaning | Aluminum |
| Color options | 10 colors | Wider variety | Vinyl |
| Cost (project total) | Slightly higher | Slightly lower | Vinyl |
| Recyclable | 100% | No | Aluminum |
| Manufacturer warranty | Up to 30 years | 10-15 years typical | Aluminum |

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/materials

---

## Service Areas

Bubbles Enterprise is based in Orlando, FL and serves all of Central Florida as the primary service area. The company also travels to South Florida for larger projects.

### Central Florida (Primary Service Area)

Orlando, Kissimmee, Winter Park, Winter Garden, Clermont, Altamonte Springs, Sanford, Oviedo, Lake Mary, Apopka, Maitland, Casselberry, Longwood, Celebration, St. Cloud, Daytona Beach, Palm Bay, Port St. Lucie.

### South Florida (Extended Service Area)

Miami, Hialeah, Fort Lauderdale, Pembroke Pines, Hollywood, Miramar, Coral Springs, Miami Gardens, West Palm Beach, Pompano Beach, Davie, Boca Raton, Sunrise, Plantation, Deerfield Beach, Miami Beach, Homestead, Boynton Beach, Kendall, Doral, North Miami, Lauderhill, Tamarac, Weston, Delray Beach, Wellington, Jupiter.

### Why Florida Homes Need Specialized Soffit

- Intense heat and humidity: Florida's average temperatures demand proper soffit ventilation to keep attics cool and prevent moisture buildup that leads to mold.
- Tropical downpours: Fascia boards must be sturdy enough to hold heavy, water-filled gutters during frequent Florida afternoon storms.
- Pest defense: Squirrels, raccoons, and wasps are common in Florida attics. Secure, sealed soffits are the first line of defense against intrusion.
- Diverse architecture: From stucco homes in South Florida to colonial styles in Orlando, the company has solutions for every home style and overhang depth.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/areas

---

## FAQ

### About Soffit & Fascia

**What is the difference between soffit and fascia?**

Soffit is the exposed surface beneath the overhanging section of a roof eave. It bridges the gap between the siding and the roofline. Fascia is the vertical finishing edge connected to the ends of the rafters, where the gutter is attached. Together, they protect the home's exterior structure and provide essential attic ventilation.

**Why is soffit ventilation so important in Florida?**

Proper soffit ventilation allows fresh air to flow into the attic while hot air escapes through ridge vents. In Florida's heat and humidity, without this airflow, moisture buildup causes mold, wood rot, and premature roof shingle failure. Good ventilation can reduce cooling costs by 15-25% and extend the roof's lifespan significantly.

**Can you repair animal damage to soffits?**

Yes. Squirrels, raccoons, rats, and birds are common in Florida attics. The company does not just patch the hole; the damaged section is repaired and the area is reinforced with durable materials to prevent future entry. These issues should be addressed immediately to avoid electrical fire risks and insulation contamination.

### Materials & Pricing

**What materials do you use?**

The company primarily uses high-quality aluminum and vinyl. Aluminum is durable, fire-resistant, low-maintenance, and lasts 20-30 years. Vinyl is cost-effective, rot-resistant, and ideal for high-moisture environments, with a lifespan around 10 years. The right material is recommended based on the home's architecture and budget.

**How much does soffit installation cost?**

Cost depends on linear footage, overhang depth, material choice (aluminum vs. vinyl), and whether it is new construction or a remove-and-replace job. Use the online calculator at bubblesenterprise.com/calculator for an instant estimate, or contact the company for a free on-site quote. Typical ranges: $6/LF for 1-story remove and replace, $7/LF for 2-story.

**How long does a soffit and fascia installation take?**

Most standard single-story homes take 1-2 days. Two-story homes and full Remove & Replace projects typically take 2-3 days. New construction is coordinated with the build timeline. A specific timeline is always provided before starting.

### Process & Policies

**What happens if rotten wood is found during installation?**

If old material is removed and rotten wood is discovered in the sub-fascia or trusses, work stops in that area immediately and the homeowner is notified. The company does not perform structural framing repairs (no framing license is held). A licensed carpenter must be hired to replace the wood before soffit work continues. This is always communicated up front with no surprise charges.

**Will gutters be removed and reinstalled?**

If gutters need to be removed to install new fascia, the old gutters will NOT be reinstalled. They deform and warp upon removal, making a proper seal impossible. A gutter company must be hired to install a new system after the soffit and fascia work is complete. The company specializes exclusively in soffit and fascia.

**What about outdoor lights, satellites, or security cameras?**

Soffit lights are removed and reinstalled as a courtesy. However, if a fixture is old and breaks during removal, or if wiring is brittle and fails, the company is not responsible for replacement. Satellite dishes and cameras are returned to their position, but signal alignment is not guaranteed; the customer's provider handles recalibration.

**How do you handle cleanup after the job?**

Cleanup includes a full sweep and a magnetic sweep for nails. If thermal insulation falls from the attic during soffit replacement, the bulk is cleaned with a blower. Fine particles in grass or rocks are unavoidable in this type of renovation and do not constitute mess left behind.

### Warranty & Payment

**What does the warranty cover?**

The company offers a 1-Year Workmanship Warranty on all installations. This covers any piece that comes loose, unclips, or falls due to an installation error. It does NOT cover damage from storms or hurricanes (Acts of God), animals chewing through the material, or damage caused by third parties modifying the work afterward.

**When is final payment due?**

The final balance is due immediately upon job completion and walkthrough, before the crew leaves the property. Deposits are required prior to scheduling. The company accepts check, Zelle, or cash. The company retains a purchase-money security interest in installed materials per Chapter 713, Florida Statutes, until full payment is received.

**What is the cancellation policy?**

If the customer cancels after materials have been purchased, a fee of 20% of the deposit value is charged to cover the supplier's restocking fee and project preparation costs. This fee is disclosed upfront and represents liquidated damages as agreed upon at booking.

**Do you offer free estimates?**

Yes. Free, no-obligation on-site estimates are provided for all soffit and fascia projects in Orlando and surrounding Central Florida areas. The property is visited, the current condition is assessed, and a transparent quote with no hidden fees is provided. An instant online estimate is also available using the calculator.

### Service Area

**What areas do you serve?**

The company serves the entire Greater Orlando area and Central Florida, including Orlando, Winter Park, Kissimmee, Altamonte Springs, Clermont, Sanford, Oviedo, Lake Mary, Apopka, and Maitland. The company also travels to South Florida for larger projects.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/faq

---

## About

Bubbles Enterprise was founded in Orlando with a clear vision: instead of being a general contractor who does everything, become the best at one thing, soffit and fascia. Orlando's climate is unforgiving. Intense heat, hurricane-force winds, and humidity demand systems built to last. The company built its expertise around exactly those conditions.

That single-minded focus is why major builders like KB Home and DR Horton trust the company, and why 500+ homeowners across Central Florida choose it by name.

### Mission

Protect and enhance Orlando homes through superior soffit and fascia with exceptional craftsmanship, premium materials, and uncompromising service.

### Promise

Every project gets full attention. The crew does not leave until the job is perfect, backed by a 100% satisfaction guarantee and warranties up to 30 years.

### Company Journey

1. Founded: Started with a simple focus on mastering soffit and fascia and nothing else.
2. First 100 Homes: Served 100+ homeowners in the Orlando area, earning 5-star reviews for precision and reliability.
3. Builder Partnerships: Selected as preferred contractor by KB Home and certified partner of DR Horton across Central Florida.
4. 500+ Projects: Over 500 homes protected. ABC Supply authorized dealer status earned. NAHB Standards certified.
5. Today: The most specialized soffit and fascia company in Central Florida.

### Core Values

- Quality Excellence: Every material, technique, and detail meets rigorous standards. No corners cut.
- Customer First: Clear communication, realistic timelines, results that exceed expectations.
- Innovation and Speed: Most projects done in 1-3 days. Efficient processes, experienced teams, zero compromise on quality.
- Transparent Pricing: No hidden fees. No surprise charges. The quote is honored from first call to final sign-off.
- Reliability and Trust: The crew shows up, finishes on time, and stands behind the work with warranties up to 30 years.
- Environmental Care: Sustainable materials, minimal waste, and proper ventilation systems that lower energy bills.

### Builder Partnerships and Certifications

- KB Home: Preferred Contractor
- DR Horton: Certified Partner
- ABC Supply: Authorized Dealer

### Installation Standards

- IRC Section 806: Building code compliant for roof ventilation. Minimum ventilation area of 1/150 of ventilated area. Balanced system with 40-50% upper and 50-60% lower ventilation. Screen openings between 1/16 inch and 1/4 inch.
- NAHB: National Green Building Standard (NGBS) Certified. Residential Construction Performance Guidelines. Energy efficiency and indoor air quality standards.
- Energy Star: Certified installation. Proper ventilation lowers AC costs by up to 15%, reduces attic temperature by 20-30 degrees Fahrenheit in summer, and eliminates moisture buildup that causes mold.
- Licensed and Insured: Florida state certified.

### Trusted by America's Top Home Builders

The company meets installation standards required by: DR Horton (#1 builder by volume), Lennar (2nd largest US builder), PulteGroup (3rd largest), Taylor Morrison (5th largest, America's Most Trusted), KB Home (7th largest, Energy Star Certified), Toll Brothers (luxury specialist, Fortune 500), Ryan Homes (part of NVR Inc.), and Ashton Woods (national private builder).

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/about

---

## Process

The company follows a four-step process for every project:

1. Free Inspection: The current soffit system is assessed at no charge, either in person or via photos.
2. Transparent Quote: A detailed written quote is provided with no hidden fees. Every line item is explained.
3. Expert Installation: A certified crew installs to code within 1-3 days, depending on project scope.
4. Quality Guarantee: The crew does not leave until the customer is satisfied. Warranty covers all materials and labor.

The company publishes educational content for homeowners:

- Soffit vs Fascia, What's the Difference: Plain English explanation of what each part does and why both matter for the home. URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/blog/soffit-vs-fascia-difference

---

## Fascia

Fascia is the vertical finishing edge connected to the ends of the rafters, where the gutter is attached. The company provides fascia repair and replacement services including rotted boards, gutter damage, and water damage. Free on-site estimate.

URL: https://bubblesenterprise.com/fascia-repair-orlando
